Eligibility: Michigan-based nonprofits, for-profits, local govts, tribal, educational institutions
Key Scoring Criteria: Alignment with focus areas: marketing, research, education/training, food safety, environmental sustainability, labor/workforce

Funder: MI Department of Labor and Economic Opportunity
Eligibility: Michigan employers training current or new employees; applications through local Michigan Works! agency
Key Scoring Criteria: Occupational demand, wage outcomes, employer commitment, skill gap addressed, training provider credentials

Grant Name: USDA REAP (Rural Energy for America Program)
Match Requirement: Yes – applicant covers 50% (IRA) or 75% (Farm Bill) of project cost
Eligibility: Agricultural producers (≥50% income from ag) OR rural small businesses in areas <50000 population
Key Scoring Criteria: Energy savings/production, environmental benefits, financial feasibility, project readiness; technical report required

Match Requirement: 1:1 match (100% of grant amount; cash or in-kind)
Eligibility: Independent agricultural producers, ag producer groups, farmer/rancher cooperatives, majority producer-owned businesses
Key Scoring Criteria: Work plan quality, personnel qualifications, commitment; priority for beginning/veteran/socially disadvantaged farmers and cooperatives

Grant Name: USDA Community Facilities Direct Loan & Grant
Match Requirement: Partial – grant covers 15-75% of project cost based on community income and population
Eligibility: Public bodies, community-based nonprofits, Federally Recognized Tribes; rural areas ≤20000 residents
Key Scoring Criteria: Community population size (smaller = higher priority), median household income vs. state median, essential service classification

Match Requirement: LEED required if capital campaign element
Eligibility: Nonprofits serving Kent County / Grand Rapids metro area; must align with Education, Environment, Arts/Culture, or Community Health
Key Scoring Criteria: Alignment with four focus areas, geographic service area, demonstrated community need

Eligibility: Local nonprofits focused on Grand Rapids neighborhood impact; community development and child/family focus
Key Scoring Criteria: Community impact, neighborhood improvement, child development, equity

Grant Name: USDA AFRI Foundational and Applied Science
Eligibility: U.S. colleges, universities, federal agencies, state ag experiment stations, private organizations
Key Scoring Criteria: Scientific merit, relevance to AFRI priority areas, integration of research + education + extension, American farmer benefit

Grant Name: USDA NIFA Specialty Crop Research Initiative (SCRI)
Match Requirement: Required (rate varies by award type; industry match preferred)
Eligibility: Colleges/universities, federal agencies, national labs, private sector, tribal organizations, nonprofits
Key Scoring Criteria: Systems approach, national/regional significance, integration of research + extension, specialty crop industry benefit, multi-institutional collaboration

Eligibility: U.S. universities and research institutions; interdisciplinary team required
Key Scoring Criteria: Genuine FEW systems integration, interdisciplinary approach, computational/modeling component, cyber-enabled decision support

Eligibility: State, local, tribal governments in partnership with community-based nonprofit; local government set-aside $20M
Key Scoring Criteria: Disadvantaged community focus, measurable environmental/health impact, workforce development reducing GHG, partnership with CBO

Eligibility: Nonprofits in Kresge priority cities (primarily Detroit, Fresno, Memphis, New Orleans); EFOD-aligned organizations
Key Scoring Criteria: Equitable food systems, community-led development, climate/health equity, EFOD framework alignment

Eligibility: Michigan businesses, nonprofits, public bodies; Opportunity Zone census tracts for OZ incentives
Key Scoring Criteria: Economic impact, job creation, investment leverage, site readiness, innovation sector alignment
